briefdescription.para: This function runs a test on the System Monitor device using the driver APIs.

detaileddescription.para:
The function does the following tasks:
Initiate the System Monitor device driver instance
Run self-test on the device
Reset the device
Set up the configuration registers for single channel continuous mode for voltage channels
wait for interrupt status bit to set
print voltage values

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMon driver Instance.
is the XPAR_<SYSMON_instance>_DEVICE_ID value from xparameters.h.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

simplesect.para: This function may never return if no interrupt occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function converts the fraction part of the given floating point number (after the decimal point)to an integer.

simplesect.para: Integer number to a precision of 3 digits.

This function is used in the printing of floating point data to a STDIO device using the xil_printf function. The xil_printf is a very small foot-print printf function and does not support the printing of floating point numbers.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 Addr, u64 *Val, u32 Condition, u64 TimeOutUs)
memberdef.name: XSysmonPsu_Poll_timeout

briefdescription.para: This function polls an address periodically until a condition is met or till the timeout occurs.

detaileddescription.para: The minimum timeout for calling this macro is 100us. If the timeout is less than 100us, it still waits for 100us. Also the unit for the timeout is 100us. If the timeout is not a multiple of 100us, it waits for a timeout of the next usec value which is a multiple of 100us.

simplesect.para: 0 - when the condition is met -1 - when the condition is not met till the timeout period

briefdescription.para: Main function that invokes the Single Channel AMS polled example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the driver functions of the System Monitor driver.

detaileddescription.para: This example here shows the usage of the driver/device in single channel sequencer off mode to measure AMS block voltages.
This code assumes that no Operating System is being used.
The value of the on-chip Vccint voltage is read from the device and then the alarm thresholds are set in such a manner that the alarm occurs.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 mn 12/13/17 First release 2.4 mn 04/26/18 Remove usleeps from AMS CTRL example 2.5 aad 08/26/19 Added local timeout poll function for IAR compiler support 2.9 cog 07/20/23 Added support for SDT flow.
